free_text: The alpha detectors were lithium drifted
silicon, used in an array with a 4 deg. spacing
between detectors. The full angular resolution of
each detector was usually 1 deg., although some
forward-angle runs on nickel and tin were done with
an angular resolution of 4 deg. The gamma detector
used was a 7.62 by 7.62-cm sodium iodide crystal.
All of the targets were self-supporting, about 1 mg/cm2
thick, and except for 12C were isotopically enriched.
No information on source of uncertainties.
No information on source of uncertainties.
